iti29:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti29:2.1: “Endowed with two things, monks, a monk lives in ease in the present life–untroubled, undistressed,
iti29:2.2: & unfeverish–and at the break-up of the body, after death, a good destination can be expected.
iti29:2.4: A guarding of the doors of the sense faculties, and knowing moderation in food.
iti29:2.5: Endowed with these two things, a monk lives in ease in the present life–untroubled, undistressed,
iti29:2.6: & unfeverish–and at the break-up of the body, after death, a good destination can be expected.”
iti29:3.3: when a monk has these doors well guarded
iti29:4.4: ease in body, ease in mind.
iti29:5.3: whether by day or by night,
iti29:5.4: one like this lives in ease.
